data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **Package references**: Replace the three separate v2 packages (`xunit.abstractions`, `xunit.extensibility.core`, `xunit.extensibility.execution`) with the single v3 package `xunit.v3.extensibility`. The runner package `xunit.runner.visualstudio` should be replaced with `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 or the new runner. Actually, let me check what the v3 packages are.

In xUnit.net v3:


- `xunit.abstractions` was merged into core
- `xunit.extensibility.core` and `xunit.extensibility.execution` combine into `xunit.v3.extensibility`
- `xunit.runner.visualstudio` updates to v3 (3.x)
- `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` remains for VS Test integration

The main packages are `xunit.v3` for general use (which bundles core, assert, and analyzers) or `xunit.v3.extensibility` for extensibility projects.

For `ITestOutputHelper`, it's moved from `Xunit.Abstractions` to the `Xunit` namespace in v3. Since this is an extensibility project, I'd need `xunit.v3.extensibility` as the replacement package.

For the code changes, I can remove the `using Xunit.Abstractions;` statement since `ITestOutputHelper` is now in the already-imported `Xunit` namespace. For `global.json`, xUnit v3 has its own built-in runner, but `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 still works for VS Test integration, so the configuration might not need major changes—though I should verify the exact package requirements for v3. Now I'm confirming the actual v3 package structure on NuGet and mapping out the migration path. I need to replace the old extensibility packages with `xunit.v3.extensibility`, update the Visual Studio runner to v3, and remove the `Xunit.Abstractions` using statement since the interfaces have moved. I'm realizing this is actually a utility library rather than a test project itself—`OutputLogger` is just a helper wrapper around `ITestOutputHelper`, so the xUnit v3 migration considerations are different than for an actual test assembly.

For an extensibility project like this, I need to swap out the three v2 packages for the single `xunit.v3.extensibility` package, update the runner configuration, and adjust any namespaces in the code accordingly. Since the project targets net8.0, that's already compatible with xUnit v3's .NET 6.0+ requirement, so I should be able to proceed with the migration using the latest stable v3 versions.

Let me check NuGet for the exact version numbers to use.

data.reasoningText: OK so `xunit.v3.extensibility` doesn't exist. Let me check what packages are available for xUnit v3. Let me look for `xunit.v3` and related packages.

OK, so the xUnit.net v3 packages follow a different versioning scheme. The latest stable versions are at 3.2.2. Let me check if there's an `xunit.v3.extensibility.execution` package too, and understand the package structure better.

Looking at the version numbers, it seems like xUnit.net v3 actually uses versions like 1.0.0, 2.0.0, 3.0.0, 3.2.2 etc. for its own packages. These are the xunit.v3 package versions, not the same as the xunit v2 2.x version numbers.
 
So for this extensibility project, I need to map the v2 packages to their v3 equivalents—`xunit.extensibility.core` and `xunit.extensibility.execution` become `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` and `xunit.v3.extensibility.execution`, while `xunit.abstractions` gets rolled into the v3 packages. Let me verify whether that execution package actually exists.

Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3.extensibility.core/3.2.2:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3 \[Core Unit Testing Framework Extensibility\]

Includes the core library (xunit.v3.core.dll), to be used for extensibility; test authors should include xunit.v3.core instead. Supports .NET Standard 2.0.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

9.9M

[Xunit.Combinatorial](/packages/Xunit.Combinatorial/)

Adds pairwise and combinatorial testing to Xunit. When combined with Xunit's \[Theory\] attribute, generates test cases that run a test under many conditions that you control via test method parameters.

6.2M

[Xunit.StaFact](/packages/Xunit.StaFact/)

Run your xunit-based tests on an STA thread with the WPF Dispatcher, a WinForms SynchronizationContext, or even a cross-platform generic UI thread emulation with a SynchronizationContext that keeps code running on a "main thread" for that test. Simply use \[WpfFact\], \[WinFormsFact\], \[StaFact\] or the cross-platform \[UIFact\] on your test method to run your test under conditions that most closely match the main thread in your application. Theory variants of these attributes allow for parameterized testing. Check out the xunit.combinatorial

## xUnit.net v3 \[Core Unit Testing Framework Extensibility\]

Includes the core library (xunit.v3.core.dll), to be used for extensibility; test authors should include xunit.v3.core instead. Supports .NET Standard 2.0.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3.extensibility.core/3.2.2:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3 \[Core Unit Testing Framework Extensibility\]

Includes the core library (xunit.v3.core.dll), to be used for extensibility; test authors should include xunit.v3.core instead. Supports .NET Standard 2.0.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

data.content:
Good, I have the full picture. Let me now apply the migration:

1. **Packages**: Replace `xunit.abstractions` + `xunit.extensibility.core` + `xunit.extensibility.execution` → `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` 3.2.2; update `xunit.runner.visualstudio` to 3.1.5
2. **Code**: Remove `using Xunit.Abstractions;` (types moved to `Xunit` namespace)

arguments.old_str:
<ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.abstractions" Version="2.0.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.core" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.extensibility.execution" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" Version="3.2.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="3.1.5" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. For xUnit.net v3:

1. The v2 packages `xunit.extensibility.core` + `xunit.extensibility.execution` + `xunit.abstractions` should be replaced with `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` (version 3.2.2 is latest stable)
2. `xunit.runner.visualstudio` should be updated to v3 (3.1.5 is latest stable)
3. `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` can stay

For the code:
- `Xunit.Abstractions` namespace no longer exists. `ITestOutputHelper` is now in `Xunit` namespace.


- In v3, the execution package has been consolidated, so `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` handles what used to be split across multiple packages, with shared types coming from `xunit.v3.common`.

Now I'm mapping out the specific changes needed: updating the project file to swap out the old xUnit packages for `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` 3.2.2 and bumping the test runner, removing the old Abstractions import from the discoverer, and checking whether the global.json runner configuration needs adjustment for v3 compatibility. I'm realizing this project is set up as a test project with the xUnit runner and test SDK packages, but it's actually just an extensibility library without any runnable tests—it's missing the `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` that xUnit v3 test projects need to generate an executable. I'm settling on a straightforward approach: replace the three v2 extensibility packages with the single `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` package, update the runner to v3, and then fix any code that breaks from the migration.

Now I'm pinning specific versions—`xunit.v3.extensibility.core` 3.2.2 and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 3.1.5—before I make the actual changes.

    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />

  </ItemGroup>

  </ItemGroup>

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.v3.extensibility.core" Version="3.2.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="3.1.5" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

using Xunit;

namespace MyApp.Tests;

public class OutputLogger
{
    private readonly ITestOutputHelper _output;

    public OutputLogger(ITestOutputHelper output)
    {
        _output = output;
    }

    public void Log(string message) => _output.WriteLine(message);
}

data.content:
Build succeeds with zero warnings and zero errors.

**Summary of changes:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `TestProject.csproj` | Replaced `xunit.abstractions` 2.0.3, `xunit.extensibility.core` 2.9.3, `xunit.extensibility.execution` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3.extensibility.core` 3.2.2; updated `xunit.runner.visualstudio` 2.8.2 → 3.1.5 |
| `CustomDiscoverer.cs` | Removed `using Xunit.Abstractions;` — `ITestOutputHelper` now lives in the `Xunit` namespace |
